title: Attestplane lease_lifecycle_event payload (v1)
description: ACTIVE — accepted under ADR-0009 (Phase 2 P0.1 + A.7). Payload-only schema for the `lease_lifecycle_event` audit event_type. This schema describes ONLY the `payload` slot of an `AuditEvent` (per ADR-0002 / types.py). The `ChainedEvent` shape is unchanged and frozen — INV 2. This is a **schema-shape re-issue** (Mode A.6) of fields originally observed at `~/aios/crates/aios-sdk-evidence/src/artifact.rs#L28-L43` (ArtifactDescriptor) + `~/aios/schemas/lease/lease.schema.json#L1-L84`. Authority-bearing fields (signature / capability_required / budget_cap / HMAC payload construction / lease-issuance verb) are explicitly NOT absorbed — see ADR-0009 § 1 Mode A.6 and the absorption-map MD row A.7. Attestplane substrate does NOT issue leases; this event records that a lease lifecycle change was OBSERVED by an external runtime.

lease_event_schema_version.description: Independent of chain.schema_version / anchor_schema_version / signature_schema_version / reason_code_schema_version. Bump requires ADR amendment.

lease_id_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of the opaque lease identifier emitted by the external authority that granted the lease. Attestplane NEVER stores the raw lease_id (it could enable replay or correlation against an external authority's secrets).

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle transition observed at observed_at. Substrate does not interpret transition validity — the authority that granted the lease is the source of truth.

grantor_runtime_id.description: Optional caller-asserted identifier of the runtime that granted the lease. NOT validated by substrate; recorded as-is. Useful for `attestplane verify` to confirm the recorded grantor matches the trust-roots entry, but the check is downstream-verifier responsibility, not substrate.

tenant_id_ref.description: Optional opaque reference to a tenant; the substrate has no multi-tenant authority (REDLINE C.6). This field exists only to round-trip caller-provided tenant identifiers for downstream correlation.

step_id_ref.description: Optional opaque reference to a step in the caller's execution graph. Substrate does not interpret.

run_id_ref.description: Optional opaque reference to a run in the caller's execution graph. Substrate does not interpret.

artifact_hash_ref.description: Optional SHA-256 hex of an artifact this lease lifecycle event relates to. Hash-only; substrate never stores the artifact body.

observed_at.description: Caller-asserted RFC 3339 timestamp of the observation. Substrate records as-is; for authoritative time, see AnchorRecord (ADR-0003 / ADR-0006).

reason_code.description: Optional machine-readable reason code drawn from the ReasonCodeV1 enum (ADR-0010, future). Free-text reason MAY be carried separately in `reason_text` for backward compatibility.

reason_text.description: Optional human-readable explanation. Backward-compat field paralleling reason_code. Verifiers SHOULD prefer reason_code for programmatic logic.

redaction_policy.$comment: Documentation block: per ADR-0009 § 1 Mode A.6 + ADR-0004 § 2 column-3, the following AIOS-originated field names MUST NOT appear in this payload. Validators SHOULD reject any unknown field (`additionalProperties: false` enforces this).

examples.reason_text: Lease granted by external policy engine; substrate records observation only.
